methods.description: This method involves constructing an age model for the U1361A Pliocene material using linear interpolation between paleomagnetic tie points. This approach is used to date the material and understand the timing and sequences of deglacial events in the Wilkes Subglacial Basin.

steps.description: Linear interpolation between paleomagnetic tie points is used to date U1361A Pliocene material following Tauxe et al. (2012) and Patterson et al. (2014).
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear interpolation between paleomagnetic tie points to date material

steps.description: Avoid areas of high core disturbance from either drilling or bioturbation.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out areas of high core disturbance

steps.description: Sample for sediment provenance and biogenic silica concentrations (wt% BSi) at sub-orbital resolution (∼10 cm sampling corresponding to ∼2-4 kyr resolution).
steps.searchable_summary: sample for sediment provenance and biogenic silica concentrations

steps.description: Constrain high-resolution intervals by at least one direct paleomagnetic datum within the depth interval analyzed for provenance.
steps.searchable_summary: validate intervals with direct paleomagnetic datum
